problem: Why do traumatic memories become locked and intrusive — what neural mechanism?
mechanism: Synaptic plasticity modulation via NMDA receptor
hypothesis: Why do traumatic memories become locked and intrusive — what neural mechanism? remains unsolved because previous research missed the role of Synaptic. Synaptic plasticity modulation via NMDA receptor directly addresses this.

experiment.protocol: Human TMS-EEG study with 12 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: What we observe is not nature itself but nature exposed to our method of questioning. — Heisenberg
